notes.1: Fine, very fine, and solid silver are mutually exclusive.
notes.2: Meteoric, orichalcum, and solid silver are mutually exclusive – although meteoric and orichalcum weapons can be silver-coated.

items.name: Balanced
items.effects: +1 to skill with a melee or thrown weapon, or a projectile – or +1 Acc for a blowpipe, bow, or crossbow. Off limits for fist loads and sticks.
items.search_name: balanced 1 to skill with a melee or thrown weapon or a projectile or 1 acc for a blowpipe bow or crossbow off limits for fist loads and sticks

items.name: Bow-Harp
items.effects: A bow that can be used as either a weapon or a harp on any given turn. Used by bards. Adds 1.5 lbs. to bow weight.
items.search_name: bow harp a bow that can be used as either a weapon or a harp on any given turn used by bards adds 1 5 lbs to bow weight

items.name: Climber’s
items.effects: Blade, prong, and/or handle design lets you climb with the weapon ready. Gives +1 to Climbing skill (a pair still gives just +1).
items.allowed_on: Any hatchet, jutte, pick, sai, sickle, tonfa, or one-handed axe
items.search_name: climber s blade prong and or handle design lets you climb with the weapon ready gives 1 to climbing skill a pair still gives just 1

items.name: Dwarven
items.effects: Changes a Parry of 0U to 0, letting a weapon that can’t normally parry and attack on the same turn do just that. Doesn’t prevent the weapon from becoming unready after attacking (3 on ST).
items.search_name: dwarven changes a parry of 0u to 0 letting a weapon that can t normally parry and attack on the same turn do just that doesn t prevent the weapon from becoming unready after attacking 3 on st

items.name: Elven
items.effects: Lets a bow shoot at +2 to ST for range and damage purposes; e.g., a ST 11 elf could draw a ST 13 bow.
items.search_name: elven lets a bow shoot at 2 to st for range and damage purposes e g a st 11 elf could draw a st 13 bow

items.name: Fine
items.effects: -1 to odds of breakage (Parrying Heavy Weapons, Exploits, p. 49) and +1 to cutting and impaling damage for a melee or thrown weapon, or a projectile – or +20% to 1/2D and Max for a blowpipe, bow, or crossbow.
items.allowed_on: Projectiles, and crushing- or impaling-only melee or thrown weapons: +2 CF. Fencing weapons (p. 81), swords (pp. 81-82), blowpipes, bows, and crossbows: +3 CF. Other cutting melee or thrown weapons: +9 CF.
items.search_name: fine 1 to odds of breakage parrying heavy weapons exploits p 49 and 1 to cutting and impaling damage for a melee or thrown weapon or a projectile or 20 to 1 2d and max for a blowpipe bow or crossbow

items.name: Meteoric
items.effects: Meteoric iron is immune to magic – Bladeturning, Missile Shield, Steel wraith, and so on won’t stop it, and Making and Breaking spells can’t destroy it. It can’t be magical, compose a power item, or benefit from weapon-enhancing spells or magical repairs.
items.search_name: meteoric meteoric iron is immune to magic bladeturning missile shield steel wraith and so on won t stop it and making and breaking spells can t destroy it it can t be magical compose a power item or benefit from weapon enhancing spells or magical repairs

items.name: Orichalcum
items.effects: Orichalcum weapons won’t break. Non-orichalcum weapons have +2 to odds of breakage when parrying heavy orichalcum ones.
items.search_name: orichalcum orichalcum weapons won t break non orichalcum weapons have 2 to odds of breakage when parrying heavy orichalcum ones

items.name: Ornate
items.effects: Jewels, gold, etc. Improves reactions from buyers (and gullible hirelings!).
items.allowed_on: All weapons except projectiles and sticks
items.cost_factor: +1 to reactions for +1 CF, +2 for +4 CF, or +3 for +9 CF.
items.search_name: ornate jewels gold etc improves reactions from buyers and gullible hirelings

items.name: Silver
items.effects: Metal melee weapons, thrown weapons, or projectiles can be made of solid silver pure enough to exploit monster vulnerabilities, but have +2 to odds of breakage: +19 CF. Silver coating for such weapons doesn’t worsen breakage, but affects monsters less (¥2 injury becomes ¥1.5, ¥3 becomes ¥2, and ¥4 becomes ¥3): +2 CF.
items.allowed_on: Metal melee weapons, thrown weapons, or projectiles
items.search_name: silver metal melee weapons thrown weapons or projectiles can be made of solid silver pure enough to exploit monster vulnerabilities but have 2 to odds of breakage 19 cf silver coating for such weapons doesn t worsen breakage but affects monsters less 2 injury becomes 1 5 3 becomes 2 and 4 becomes 3 2 cf

items.name: Very Fine
items.effects: -2 to odds of breakage and +2 to cutting and impaling damage.
items.search_name: very fine 2 to odds of breakage and 2 to cutting and impaling damage

items.name: Balanced
items.effects: +1 to Shield skill.
items.search_name: balanced 1 to shield skill

items.name: Dwarven
items.effects: Hefty iron instead of wood. Adds +1 damage with a shield bash or rush (cumulative with +1 for a spike) and makes the shield fireproof. Doubles weight!
items.search_name: dwarven hefty iron instead of wood adds 1 damage with a shield bash or rush cumulative with 1 for a spike and makes the shield fireproof doubles weight

items.name: Fine
items.effects: Shield has the same DB at only 3/4 of its usual weight.
items.search_name: fine shield has the same db at only 3 4 of its usual weight

items.name: Meteoric
items.effects: Resists magic as for the weapon modifier (p. 106), adds +1 damage with a shield bash or rush (cumulative with +1 for a spike), and is fireproof. Doubles weight!
items.search_name: meteoric resists magic as for the weapon modifier p 106 adds 1 damage with a shield bash or rush cumulative with 1 for a spike and is fireproof doubles weight

items.name: Mirrored
items.effects: Polished or silvered to reflect gaze attacks. A successful Block against such an attack allows an immediate Shield roll to redirect the curse at anyone within its usual range. If the gaze allows a resistance roll, the target of a reflected gaze resists at +3.
items.allowed_on: Dwarven, meteoric, or orichalcum shields: +2 CF. Other shields: +6 CF.
items.search_name: mirrored polished or silvered to reflect gaze attacks a successful block against such an attack allows an immediate shield roll to redirect the curse at anyone within its usual range if the gaze allows a resistance roll the target of a reflected gaze resists at 3

items.name: Orichalcum
items.effects: Shield has the same DB at only 2/3 of its usual weight, gives non-orichalcum weapons +2 to odds of breakage when parrying a shield bash or rush, and is fire proof.
items.search_name: orichalcum shield has the same db at only 2 3 of its usual weight gives non orichalcum weapons 2 to odds of breakage when parrying a shield bash or rush and is fire proof

items.name: Ornate
items.effects: Also allowed on cloaks. Impresses plebes as for the weapon modifier (p. 106): +1 to reactions for +1 CF, +2 for +4 CF, or +3 for +9 CF.
items.search_name: ornate also allowed on cloaks impresses plebes as for the weapon modifier p 106 1 to reactions for 1 cf 2 for 4 cf or 3 for 9 cf

items.name: Dragonhide
items.effects: Heavy leather armor (DR 2) can be dragonhide. This provides from +1 to +4 DR, depending on the dragon, and double that DR bonus against fire. (Dragonhide with up to +7 DR – and unusual dragonhide that doubles its bonus vs. acid, cold, or other damage – exists but isn’t for sale.) It also gives -3 reactions from dragons! Weight and cost depend on DR bonus: +1 DR (+2 vs. fire) is 1.25¥ weight and +29 CF; +2 DR (+4 vs. fire) is 1.5¥ weight and +35 CF; +3 DR (+6 vs. fire) is 1.75¥ weight and +41 CF; and +4 DR (+8 vs. fire) is 2¥ weight and +47 CF.
items.search_name: dragonhide heavy leather armor dr 2 can be dragonhide this provides from 1 to 4 dr depending on the dragon and double that dr bonus against fire dragonhide with up to 7 dr and unusual dragonhide that doubles its bonus vs acid cold or other damage exists but isn t for sale it also gives 3 reactions from dragons weight and cost depend on dr bonus 1 dr 2 vs fire is 1 25 weight and 29 cf 2 dr 4 vs fire is 1 5 weight and 35 cf 3 dr 6 vs fire is 1 75 weight and 41 cf and 4 dr 8 vs fire is 2 weight and 47 cf

items.name: Dwarven
items.effects: Dwarves can forge DR 6-9 plate armor (note [3]) to be harder! This gives +1 DR without raising weight.
items.search_name: dwarven dwarves can forge dr 6 9 plate armor note 3 to be harder this gives 1 dr without raising weight

items.name: Elven
items.effects: Elven mail uses the higher DR listed for the base armor against all damage – don’t reduce its DR vs. crushing attacks.
items.search_name: elven elven mail uses the higher dr listed for the base armor against all damage don t reduce its dr vs crushing attacks

items.name: Fine
items.effects: Expertly fitted, with no waste material. Offers full DR at 3/4 of the usual weight. Only fits wearers whose height and weight match the original owner’s!

items.name: Giant-Spider Silk
items.effects: Improves light cloth armor (DR 1) to DR 2 without raising weight – and allows it to be worn under other armor (+2 DR) without -1 to DX for layering.

items.effects: Dress to impress, exactly as for the weapon modifier (p. 106).

items.name: Spiked
items.effects: Every turn, roll DX-4 to stab each enemy in close combat with you as a free action; success deals 1d-2 impaling damage. In addition, anyone who strikes such armor with an unarmed attack takes this damage to the attacking body part – and if the attack is a bite, grapple, or anything under Slams, Tramples, and Overruns (Exploits, p. 40), the attacker suffers maximum damage (4 points).
items.allowed_on: Any plate armor (note [3]) that covers the body or more
items.search_name: spiked every turn roll dx 4 to stab each enemy in close combat with you as a free action success deals 1d 2 impaling damage in addition anyone who strikes such armor with an unarmed attack takes this damage to the attacking body part and if the attack is a bite grapple or anything under slams tramples and overruns exploits p 40 the attacker suffers maximum damage 4 points

items.name: Thieves’
items.effects: Blackened mail woven for maximum flexibility, minimum noise. Find the total weight of your gear with and without this armor, and use weight without to figure encumbrance penalties (p. 12) to Acrobatics, Climbing, Stealth, and other skills (but not to Move or Dodge).
items.search_name: thieves blackened mail woven for maximum flexibility minimum noise find the total weight of your gear with and without this armor and use weight without to figure encumbrance penalties p 12 to acrobatics climbing stealth and other skills but not to move or dodge
